proxy.title: Proxy Responses
proxy.subtitle: Records of Lead replying on your behalf · {{count}} items
proxy.helpText: When you're offline beyond the threshold, Lead agent answers pending questions on your behalf. Side-effect decisions (e.g., dispatching workflows) are conservatively declined by default. You can retract any proxy response. These settings can be adjusted in Settings → Personal Settings under 'Lead Proxy'.

channels.title: Messaging channels
channels.subtitle: Link Holons to an IM client so Lead can reach you — and you can reach Lead — from your phone.
channels.confirmUnbind: Unbind this bot from your Holons account?
channels.awaitStart: Bot ready — send /start to your bot to finish the link.
channels.webhookSetPrompt: Set a public URL below so the platform can push updates here.
channels.webhookSetupHint: Your Holons deployment needs a publicly reachable https:// URL so the platform can deliver messages.

telegram.desc: Personal Telegram bot. Defaults to polling (zero infra). Webhook mode available once you have a public URL.
telegram.step1: Talk to @BotFather in Telegram and run /newbot to create a new bot.
telegram.step2: Copy the HTTP API token BotFather gives you.
telegram.step3: Paste it below. Once saved, open your bot in Telegram and send /start.

slack.desc: Slack workspace bot. Webhook-only — requires a public URL for Slack's Events API.
slack.step1: Create a Slack app at api.slack.com/apps and add a Bot User.
slack.step2: Under OAuth & Permissions, install to your workspace — copy the Bot User OAuth Token (xoxb-...).
slack.step3: Paste the token below. After saving, switch transport to webhook and configure Slack's Event Subscriptions URL.

line.desc: LINE Messaging API bot. Webhook-only — requires a public URL for LINE's callback.
line.step1: Create a Messaging API channel at developers.line.biz.
line.step2: In the Messaging API tab, issue a long-lived Channel access token and copy it.
line.step3: Paste the token below. After saving, set your webhook URL in the LINE console.

personal.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 4 characters
personal.leadWorkflowDesc: These settings control how Lead agent designs workflows for you. Lead will respect these limits when proposing workflow structures.
personal.leadMaxStepsHint: Maximum number of nodes (including group members) Lead can put in a single workflow. If a task requires more steps, Lead will tell you and suggest simplifications.
personal.leadMaxTokensHint: Estimated maximum total tokens for a workflow run. Each agent node uses roughly 3,000–5,000 tokens. Lead will warn you if the design exceeds this budget.
personal.leadHowItWorksContent:
When you ask Lead to plan a workflow, it:
1. Analyzes your request and breaks it into steps
2. Checks each agent's current workload (queue depth)
3. Assigns the best-fit agent for each step — if the specialist is busy, it may assign an idle agent with a temporary role override
4. Respects parallelism: independent steps run simultaneously
5. Stays within your max steps and token budget

You can always edit the proposed workflow before running it.
personal.autoTopupDesc: If a scheduled trigger wants to run but the agent's daily cost cap has been hit, grant a small automatic extension instead of failing. Off by default. A global hard cap still applies.

library.credentialHintDb: Stored in database with Fernet encryption.

library.hintMcp: Model Context Protocol servers — default entries (Google Drive etc.) are templates only: URL is empty and disabled. Admin needs to click 'Detail' to fill in the actual endpoint & credential before enabling.

noModel.title: No usable model connection
noModel.noneExist: You haven't set up any LLM connection yet. At least one working connection is required before any agent can run.
noModel.noneUsable: Every model connection is either missing a credential or disabled. Configure one or re-enable one.
noModel.allFailing: Every model connection failed its last test. Fix at least one so agents can make LLM calls.

backup.title: Backup & export
backup.subtitle: Before upgrading, download a snapshot of your local database. Your data lives at ~/.agent_company/ and is not deleted when the app is replaced — but a manual backup is your safety net.
backup.restoreHow: How to restore from a backup
backup.restoreStep1: Quit the Holons app completely (Cmd-Q, not just close the window).
backup.restoreStep2: Move your downloaded .db file to ~/.agent_company/data.db (overwrite the existing one).
backup.restoreStep3: Remove the WAL sidecars if present: rm -f ~/.agent_company/data.db-shm ~/.agent_company/data.db-wal
backup.restoreStep4: Re-open the Holons app. The new data is loaded on startup.

projects.coordinatorHint: Coordinator plans workflows within the project, respecting each member's allocation.
